titleStmt.title: The High Commissioner in Haiti ( (Russell) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The High Commissioner in Haiti ( Russell ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 106. All quiet. Reports indicate conditions throughout Haiti steadily improving.
No present evidence of unrest in central Haiti. Prominent Haitians at Cape Haitien state that they welcome a firm orderly government whether administered by Americans or their own Government.

Reassuring statements by garde are disseminated quieting the country people. Have requested Galveston to visit Aux Cayes, Jeremie,
and Miragoane then to Port-au-Prince for fresh provisions if so desired. If conditions continue to improve as now seems highly probable, I shall release Galveston on arrival Port-au-Prince as desired by this week. Three-fourths of the school children at Cape Haitien have returned to school.

Every one of the points brought out in the above statement is directly contrary to facts and farcical. One of the signers was Minister of the Interior under Guillaume Sam when 167 political prisoners were murdered in the prison.
